name: Pear-Plum Spread
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and core the pears; chop coarsely.
2. Place the chopped pears in a large saucepan and sprinkle with ascorbic acid color keeper.
3. Remove pits from plums and cut each plum into 8 pieces.
4. Stir the plums into the pears.
5. Wrap any remaining pits, cloves, and ginger (if used) in cheesecloth and place under some of the fruit.
6. Add cinnamon stick and water to the saucepan.
7. Cover and bring to a boil, then simmer for 15 to 20 minutes or until the fruit is tender.
8. Discard the spice bag and cinnamon stick.
9. Puree the fruit mixture in a blender or food processor, or press through a sieve.
10. Return the pureed mixture to the saucepan.
11. Simmer uncovered for an additional 15 to 20 minutes, or until the mixture coats a metal spoon.
12. Remove from heat and stir in sweetener.
13. Ladle into hot clean jars, leaving 1/2 inch headspace.
14. Wipe jar rims, seal, and process for 10 minutes in a boiling water bath.
15. Store in a cool, dark, dry place. Processing may be omitted if the spreads are to be used within a short period of time. Store in the refrigerator up to 1 month or freezer up to 3 months.
Makes about 3 1/4 cups, with each serving containing 1 tablespoon, approximately 3 grams of carbohydrates, and 12 calories.
